danger_signals.visual_form: Evil-aligned creatures (Sauron, Chaos, Diablo, Vertigo) have darker, more menacing visual designs
danger_signals.meaning: Signals threat level and destructive intent toward world stability

danger_signals.visual_form: Ruined stages (Inferno, Ruins, Tomb) with volcanic, decay, and death imagery
danger_signals.meaning: World in state of destruction; danger from environment and chaos forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Human worshippers visible in arenas, wandering unprotected
danger_signals.meaning: Civilians at risk; creatures can consume them, indicating danger to human civilization
Visual feedback of immediate danger and combat pressure

safety_signals.visual_form: Blizzard (ape god of good) and Armadon (ceratopian god of life) have protective, less aggressive designs
safety_signals.meaning: Forces exist that maintain peace and preserve life on Urth

safety_signals.visual_form: The Cliff (Blizzard), the Hollows (Armadon), the Strip (Talon) environments appear more stable
safety_signals.meaning: Domains of stabilizing forces; safer territories

safety_signals.visual_form: Tribesman congregate and worship nearby creatures
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization structure exists; social order maintained through deity worship

safety_signals.visual_form: Player can eat tribesman to restore health during match
safety_signals.meaning: Symbiotic relationship between gods and humans provides sustenance and stability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Protective/life-associated creature forms (ape god of good, life-giving ceratopian, survival raptor)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposed by chaos and evil creatures with menacing designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: The Cliff (Blizzard's domain), the Hollows (Armadon's domain), the Strip (Talon's domain) - relatively stable, less chaotic visuals

faction_indicators.faction: Forces of Evil/Chaos (Sauron, Chaos, Diablo, Vertigo)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Destructive/chaotic creature forms (tyrannosaurs of hunger/evil, ape god of decay, cobra goddess of insanity)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposed by good-aligned deities; more aggressive visual presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: The Inferno (Diablo's domain), the Ruins (Chaos's domain), the Tomb (Vertigo's domain) - visually chaotic, decayed, violent

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Stone Age dwellers; segregated clans form beneath their chosen deity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vulnerable to any creature; prey to evil gods seeking power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present in all arenas; their faction identity determined by which god they worship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urth's continental landmass shaped like fire-breathing dinosaur skull; primordial rainforest covering land; meteor strike aftermath visible in ruined environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humanity regressed to Stone Age tribes; civilization collapsed; survivors form clan-based worship structures around emerged creatures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven supernatural creatures appear, each with distinct visual identity matching their domain and godly function
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humans immediately begin worshipping these creatures as gods; social reorganization around deity alignment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Good-aligned creatures (Blizzard, Armadon) present in relatively stable environments; evil-aligned creatures (Sauron, Chaos, Diablo) in chaotic/destructive domains
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures battle to determine planet's fate; human tribes divided by allegiance; outcome determines whether Urth maintains peace or descends into chaos

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Victory creature performs finishing move; defeated creature eliminated from consideration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winning deity's influence expands; their tribe gains dominance; human worshippers align with victor

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player creature faces all six opponents sequentially with increased power; final arena design reflects ultimate battle stakes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single creature emerges dominant; determines Urth's ultimate fate (peace or chaos dominion)

environmental_storytelling.example: Seven stages correspond to seven creatures' domains: The Inferno (Diablo = evil), The Ruins (Chaos = decay), The Tomb (Vertigo = madness/death) vs. The Cliff (Blizzard = stability), The Hollows (Armadon = life/protection). Visual design of each stage reinforces the creature's ideological alignment.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Creature Visual Design as Moral Alignment
environmental_storytelling.example: Good creatures (Blizzard as protective ape, Armadon as life-sustaining ceratopian) have less aggressive visual language. Evil creatures (Sauron/Diablo as hunger-driven tyrannosaurs, Chaos as decay ape, Vertigo as insanity cobra) have menacing, destructive visual presentations.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Worshipper Behavior as World State Indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Tribal humans visible in arenas actively worshipping creatures; their presence and worship mechanics show that civilization persists but entirely depends on creature dominance. Consumption of worshippers shows predatory relationship between gods and humans.

Ruined, volcanic, and tomb-like environments visually communicate chaos and danger. Cliff, hollows, and natural strip environments visually communicate relative stability. Environmental state reflects each creature's influence on world.

Each creature's fatality reflects their nature: humorous/transformative (Vertigo), predatory (tyrannosaurs), decaying (Chaos). Finishing move choreography visually reinforces creature identity and world-state implications of victory.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Protective stance; stage environment relatively stable; worshippers maintain organized presence
Life-sustaining presence; stage shows natural growth; worshippers appear safe and organized
Predatory but stabilizing; represents primal survival rather than chaos; stage relatively functional
Aggressive, consuming behavior; actively hunts worshippers; stage shows predatory dominance imagery
Destructive, entropic behavior; stage environment visually decays; worshippers in danger
Unpredictable, reality-warping behavior (fatality transforms opponents into animals); stage reflects madness; worshippers visibly distressed
Purely destructive, chaotic intent; stage environment infernal/volcanic; represents ultimate threat to Urth stability
Wander arenas worshipping nearby creature; can be consumed for health; behavior differs based on proximity to good vs. evil aligned deity

notes: Primal Rage communicates world-state primarily through creature visual design, environmental stage design, and the ideological split between good-aligned stabilizing forces and evil-aligned chaotic forces. The post-apocalyptic setting (meteor-devastated Urth) provides the narrative framework, while the seven creatures' specialized domains and appearances create a visual faction system. Human worshippers serve as both environmental storytelling elements (showing civilization structure) and mechanical resources (consumable for health). The game's core conflict—whether Urth will be dominated by forces of peace/life or chaos/hunger—is visually communicated through arena design, creature appearance, and finishing move choreography. The fighting game format itself acts as mechanical storytelling: combat determines planetary fate. Good vs. evil alignment is not explicitly labeled but is visually communicated through creature design (protective vs. predatory forms, life-sustaining vs. decay-associated imagery) and stage environments (stable vs. chaotic visual language).
